name: Basel-Stadt (Switzerland)
description: A square white field charged with a black Baselstab (a stylised bishop's crosier) in the centre, the crook turned toward the hoist.
history: The Baselstab appears in the seals of the bishops of Basel from the 13th century; the black staff, for the city as distinct from the bishop's red one, was used on the city banner from the early 15th century.

features.role: Baselstab, the Basel bishop's crosier, in pale facing the hoist
features.symbolism: the crosier of the Prince-Bishopric of Basel; black for the city
